---
title: "00 · Kiselev — Arithmetic"
subtitle: "Whole numbers, divisibility, fractions, decimals, ratio, percentage — the original Russian arithmetic that trained 60 million students."
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **Kiselev Arithmetic** track — A. P. Kiselev's first
textbook, *Систематический курс арифметики* (1884), the book that
*started the legend*. Used continuously in Russian and Soviet
schools from 1884 to 1955, it taught the four operations, fractions,
ratio, and percentage to entire generations.

Arithmetic is where mathematics becomes *physical*: every operation
maps onto something a learner can *count*, *measure*, or *compare*.
Kiselev's treatment is famously concrete and famously rigorous at
the same time — he gives short, clean *proofs* of arithmetic facts
that other texts treat as obvious.

By the end of this track you can:

- Operate fluently with **whole numbers** and the four operations,
  understanding place value as a structural fact (not a memorised
  procedure).
- Apply **divisibility rules** and find **prime factorisations**,
  **GCD**, and **LCM** in seconds.
- Compute with **common fractions** (proper, improper, mixed) and
  **decimal fractions** interchangeably.
- Solve **ratio and proportion** problems — including the classical
  *rule of three*.
- Compute **percentages**, **simple interest**, and **compound
  interest**.

## Source

Russian original (public domain on archive.org):
[Систематический курс арифметики, А. П. Киселёв](https://archive.org/details/libgen_00032025)
— this track maps onto the chapter ordering of that scan.

A modern complete English translation by Valery Manokhin is available
(paid) on [Leanpub](https://leanpub.com/arithmetic) — useful if you'd
rather read every word in English; this track works without it.

## Lessons

The full Arithmetic track, in book order:

1. [**01 · Whole Numbers and the Four Operations (Ch 1)**](01-whole-numbers/index.qmd) —
   place value, addition / subtraction / multiplication / division,
   the division algorithm.
2. [**02 · Divisibility, Primes, GCD and LCM (Ch 2)**](02-divisibility/index.qmd) —
   divisibility rules, the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic,
   Euclid's algorithm.
3. [**03 · Common Fractions (Ch 3)**](03-common-fractions/index.qmd) —
   equivalent fractions, four operations, comparison via
   cross-multiplication.
4. [**04 · Decimal Fractions (Ch 4)**](04-decimals/index.qmd) —
   place value to the right; terminating vs repeating; conversion.
5. [**05 · Ratio and Proportion (Ch 5)**](05-ratio-proportion/index.qmd) —
   fundamental property, direct vs inverse, the rule of three.
6. [**06 · Percentage and Interest (Ch 6)**](06-percentage/index.qmd) —
   the three classical percentage questions, simple vs compound
   interest, the rule of $72$.

**Track complete.** Continue to
[*Algebra Part I*](../tier-kiselev-1-algebra-1/index.qmd) for
signed numbers and beyond.

## Sister synthesis tier

The synthesis lessons covering the same ground sit primarily in
[Tier 1 Foundations](../tier-1-foundations/index.qmd) — number
sense, place value, fractions, decimals, ratio, and primes. Each
Kiselev arithmetic lesson links to its specific sister synthesis
lesson.

A learner who has finished synthesis Tier 1 can use this tier as
a *deeper, proof-aware* parallel reading; a learner starting from
zero can use it as the primary path, with the synthesis tier as a
gentler companion.
